title: uentoccs
text: uentoccs Return the number of occurrences in the specified entity parameter. long uentoccs (UHACT hEnt, long* numOccs ); Parameters hEnt Handle to the entity parameter. numOccs Number of occurrences in the entity parameter (Output). Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uentoccs Value Meaning 0 Success -165 The supplied handle is not valid. Use Library: ucall Description uentoccs returns (by reference) the number of occurrences in the specified entity parameter. Example: uentoccs, uint2uf, uuf2int The following extended example demonstrates the use of uentoccs , and other entity- and parameter-related functions. The entity CUSTOMER is defined as: Explain this code CUSTNUM integer NAME char(20) ADDRESS char(20) ACCOUNTNUM integer Copy The service CUSTSRV contains two operations. One is GETCUST which has CUSTOMER as an OUT parameter. This operation creates three occurrences of CUSTOMER. The other operation is SETCUST which has CUSTOMER as an IN parameter. It prints all occurrences to the message frame. Operation GETCUST and SETCUST The operations GETCUST and SETCUST in service CUSTSRV are defined as follows: Explain this code operation getcust params entity customer : out; endparams creocc "customer", -1 CUSTNUM = 123 name = "john doe" address = "someplace" accountnum = 123456 creocc "customer", -1 CUSTNUM = 234 name = "jane doe" address = "anywhere" accountnum = 234567 creocc "customer", -1 CUSTNUM = 345 name = "flabber gast" address = "unknown" accountnum = 345678 return 0 end operation setcust params entity customer : in; endparams setocc "customer", 1 putmess "%%custnum %%name %%address %%accountnum" setocc "customer", 2 putmess "%%custnum %%name %%address %%accountnum" setocc "customer", 3 putmess "%%custnum %%name %%address %%accountnum" return 0 end Copy 3GL code Our 3GL code first activates operation SETCUST to get the entity data. It passes on this data to SETCUST, but with all STRING fields converted to uppercase. To do this, we loop through all the occurrences and fields of our output entity parameter, creating occurrences and filling fields of the input entity parameter as necessary. Finally we call SETCUST that prints the converted data to the message frame: Explain this code 123 JOHN DOE SOMEPLACE 123456 234 JANE DOE ANYWHERE 234567 345 FLABBER GAST UNKNOWN 345678 Copy Note that the 3GL code treats every field that is not a STRING as an INTEGER. This happens to be sufficient for this particular entity. A more generic approach would probably use uprmgets and uprmputs to handle all fields not of type STRING. The 3GL code follows: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <ctype.h> void convert_customer () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hPrm; /* Parameter handle */ UHACT hEnt1, hEnt2; /* Entity handles */ long numOccs, numFlds; /* Nr of occurrrences and fields */ long iOcc, iFld; /* Indices of occurrence and field */ int fType; /* Field datatype */ UCHAR* strVal; /* Allocated string buffer */ UCHAR *p; /* Work pointer */ int intVal; /* Integer value */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC*/ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"CUSTSRV", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate operation GETCUST */ /* Instantiate operation SETCUST */ uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETCUST", 0, &hOp1); uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETCUST", 0, &hOp2); uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETCUST */ uprmgeth(hOp1, 1, 0, &hEnt1); /* Get OUT handle for CUSTOMER*/ uprmgeth(hOp2, 1, 0, &hEnt2); /* Get IN handle for CUSTOMER*/ uentoccs (hEnt1, &numOccs); /* Get number of occurrences */ uentflds (hEnt1, &numFlds); /* Get number of fields */ for ( iOcc=1; iOcc<=numOccs; iOcc++ )/* Loop through occurrences */ { uentseto(hEnt1, iOcc); /* Make occurrence current */ uentcreo(hEnt2, iOcc); /* Create new occ for SETCUST */ for ( iFld=1; iFld<=numFlds; iFld++ )/* Loop through fields */ { uprmgeth(hEnt1, iFld, 0, &hPrm); /* Get field handle */ uprmtype(hPrm, &fType); /* Get field type */ if ( fType == UTYPE_STRING ) /* Handle string field */ { uuf2str(hEnt1, iFld, &strVal); /* Get string data */ for ( p=strVal; *p; p++ ) *p = toupper(*p); /* Convert to uppercase */ ustr2uf(hEnt2, iFld, strVal); /* Put converted data */ unifree(hEnt1, strVal); /* Free string buffer */ } else { uuf2int(hEnt1, iFld, &intVal);/* Get integer value */ uint2uf(hEnt2, iFld, intVal); /* Put integer value */ } ufreeh(&hPrm); /* Free parameter handle */ } } uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation SETENT */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free GETCUST operation handle */ ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free SETCUST operation handle */ ufreeh (&hEnt1); /* Free entity handle */ ufreeh (&hEnt2); /* Free entity handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Related Links uentcreo Create an occurrence of an entity parameter, and make it current. uentflds Return the number of fields in the specified entity parameter. uentseto Make an occurrence of an entity parameter current. uint2uf Copy the specified integer value to a basic parameter or entity field. ustr2uf Copy string data from a program buffer to a basic parameter or entity field. uuf2int Copy an integer value from a basic parameter or entity field to an int program variable. uuf2str Get a pointer to the string data in a basic parameter or entity field.
